location.office_time_required: 3 days per week on Mondays, Wednesdays, and Thursdays

level_signals: Minimum of 4 years of product management experience
Expected base pay range $136,160 - $170,200 in Seattle area
Owns roadmap, leads cross-functional teams, aligns with executives

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Minimum of 4 years of product management experience
Have shipped products in deeply technical and analytical domains - able to use data and experimentation to guide a team towards successful execution
Experience managing Operations Research/ML/algorithmic products across any domain
Ability to define and analyze metrics that inform the success of products
Experience running A/B tests and using common statistical techniques to evaluate results. Experience leading post-launch optimizations including developing testing strategies
Experience with data, including querying, aggregation, analysis, and visualization
Be detail-oriented and able to dive deep into complex technical concepts, but also abstract them to cross-functional stakeholders and leaders
Strong communication skills and intuition for communication strategy at every level -- within teams, within the company, to users
Natural ability to make things happen around you. You manage project ambiguity, complexity and interdependencies in an organized and structured way.
Develop and own quarterly and yearly roadmaps and financial impact for your team
Lead and align cross-functional partners and team: engineers, scientists, analysts, business partners, operations to drive a shared vision

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Maintain an objective, strong POV on what to build for our users, amidst many different stakeholders, world-class scientists, and experienced engineers with varied goals

scope.surface: Matching & Fulfillment — ETA estimation, cost of fulfillment, driver-rider matching, on-time promise, pre-request match generation, post-request fulfillment, matching core services
core_responsibilities: Own team's products from ideation through development to launch, growth and maintenance
Represent the customer's point of view and champion a customer-first approach to product development
Align on business goals for optimization with executives and business teams
Define short-term and long-term strategy to achieve those goals
Develop and own quarterly and yearly roadmaps and financial impact for your team
Write product requirements documents and review experiment design docs and technical specifications
Ideate impactful projects, break them into MVP and milestones, test and develop hypotheses through fast experimentation
Work closely with partner teams to represent your team's perspective on new initiatives
Lead and align cross-functional partners and team to drive a shared vision

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed ($136,160 - $170,200 base in Seattle area)
Clear product surface and ownership area (Matching & Fulfillment group)
Specific hybrid schedule stated (Mon/Wed/Thu) — no ambiguity
Equity and bonus mentioned in addition to base
Comprehensive benefits package described in detail
4 weeks/year remote flexibility on top of hybrid schedule
red_flags: Hard OR/ML/algorithmic background requirement is specific and may be difficult to mitigate without that technical domain
Visa sponsorship status not mentioned — unclear for international candidates
Location appears to be Seattle only — no SF or NYC option stated
Salary range is Seattle-area specific; other locations may differ but no other cities listed for this role

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range + years + responsibilities)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly listed in JD)
location.remote_us_eligible (inferred from hybrid-only language; not explicitly stated as ineligible)
location.visa_sponsorship (not mentioned in JD; marked unclear)
ambiguities: 'Maintain an objective, strong POV on what to build for our users' is listed under 'This individual should:' section (same as hard requirements) but reads more as a cultural/soft trait — classified as nice_to_have given its subjective nature rather than a measurable requirement.
The JD lists only Seattle area comp range but does not explicitly state this role is Seattle-only; other Lyft offices are not mentioned for this role.
